bgg_approx_count_distinct.syntax_info: This function is similar to COUNT(DISTINCT) in counting the number of distinct values but returns an approximate result. It uses the BJKST algorithm, significantly reducing memory consumption when processing large datasets with a power-law distribution. Moreover, for low-cardinality data, this function provides high accuracy while maintaining efficient CPU utilization. Can accept multiple expressions.

bgg_approx_percentile.syntax_info: This function returns the percentile of expr. The constant_integer_expr argument indicates the percentage value which is a constant integer in the range of [1,100]. A percentile Pk (k represents percentage) indicates that there are at least k% values in the data set that are less than or equal to Pk. This function only supports the numeric type and the date and time type as the returned type of expr. For other returned types, APPROX_PERCENTILE only returns NULL.

period_diff.syntax_info: PERIOD_DIFF(P1,P2) Returns the number of months between periods P1 and P2. P1 and P2 should be in the format YYMM or YYYYMM. Note that the period arguments P1 and P2 are not date values. This function returns NULL if P1 or P2 is NULL.

BENCHMARK.syntax_info: BENCHMARK(count, expr) executes the expression expr repeatedly count times. It always returns 0.

div_slash.syntax_info: Division operator. Returns the result of dividing one number by another.

mod_percent.syntax_info: Modulo operator. Returns the remainder of N divided by M.

assign.syntax_info: Assignment operator. Causes the user variable on the left hand side of the operator to take on the value to its right.

between.syntax_info: Checks whether a value is within a range. Syntax: expr BETWEEN min AND max. Returns 1 if expr is between min and max (inclusive), 0 otherwise.

exists.syntax_info: Returns TRUE if a subquery returns any rows, FALSE otherwise. Syntax: EXISTS (subquery).

ilike.syntax_info: Case-insensitive pattern matching. Similar to LIKE but performs case-insensitive comparison. Returns 1 or 0.

in_op.syntax_info: Checks whether a value is within a set of values. Returns 1 if the value is in the list, 0 otherwise. Syntax: expr IN (value1, value2, ...)

isnull_func.syntax_info: Tests whether a value is NULL. Returns 1 if the value is NULL, 0 if it is not NULL.

is_boolean.syntax_info: Tests whether a value is a specific boolean value. Syntax: expr IS TRUE, expr IS FALSE, or expr IS UNKNOWN. Returns 1 or 0.

is_not_boolean.syntax_info: Tests whether a value is not a specific boolean value. Syntax: expr IS NOT TRUE, expr IS NOT FALSE, or expr IS NOT UNKNOWN. Returns 1 or 0.

is_not_null.syntax_info: Tests whether a value is not NULL. Returns 1 if the value is not NULL, 0 if it is NULL.

is_null.syntax_info: IS NULL Tests whether a value is NULL. mysql> SELECT 1 IS NULL, 0 IS NULL, NULL IS NULL; -> 0, 0, 1

not_between.raw_syntax_name: expr NOT BETWEEN min AND max
not_between.syntax_info: Checks whether a value is not within a range. Syntax: expr NOT BETWEEN min AND max. Returns 1 if expr is not between min and max, 0 otherwise.

not_exists.syntax_info: Returns TRUE if a subquery returns no rows, FALSE otherwise. Syntax: NOT EXISTS (subquery).

not_in.syntax_info: Checks whether a value is not within a set of values. Returns 1 if the value is not in the list, 0 otherwise. Syntax: expr NOT IN (value1, value2, ...)

not_op.syntax_info: Logical NOT operator. Negates a boolean expression. Returns 1 if the expression is 0, 0 if the expression is non-zero, NULL if the expression is NULL.

pipes_or.syntax_info: Logical OR operator. Returns true if either operand is true.

ASCII.syntax_info: The ASCII(str) function returns the ASCII value of the leftmost character in the given argument. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

BIN.syntax_info: The BIN() function converts the given argument into a string representation of its binary value. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

BIT_LENGTH.syntax_info: The BIT_LENGTH() function returns the length of a given argument in bits. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

CHAR.syntax_info: The CHAR() function returns the character for each integer passed. If multiple arguments are supplied, they are concatenated. Returns NULL if any argument is NULL.

CHARACTER_LENGTH.syntax_info: The CHARACTER_LENGTH() function is a synonym for CHAR_LENGTH(). Returns the number of characters in a string. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

CHAR_LENGTH.syntax_info: The CHAR_LENGTH() function returns the number of characters in a string. Multi-byte characters count as a single character. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

concat.syntax_info: The CONCAT() function concatenates one or more arguments into a single string. If any argument is NULL, returns NULL.

concat_ws.syntax_info: The CONCAT_WS() function concatenates strings with a separator. Syntax: CONCAT_WS(separator, str1, str2, ...). The first argument is the separator, followed by strings to concatenate. If separator is NULL, returns NULL.

ELT.syntax_info: The ELT() function returns the element at the specified index from the list of strings. Returns NULL if index is out of range or NULL.

EXPORT_SET.syntax_info: The EXPORT_SET() function returns a string with on/off values for each bit. Syntax: EXPORT_SET(bits, on, off [, separator [, number_of_bits]]). Returns NULL if any argument is NULL.

FIELD.syntax_info: The FIELD() function returns the index position of the first argument in the subsequent arguments. Returns 0 if not found, NULL if any argument is NULL.

FIND_IN_SET.syntax_info: The FIND_IN_SET() function returns the index position of the first argument within the second argument (a comma-separated list). Returns 0 if not found, NULL if any argument is NULL.

FROM_BASE64.syntax_info: The FROM_BASE64() function decodes a Base64 encoded string and returns the decoded result in hexadecimal form. Returns NULL if argument is NULL or not valid Base64.

INSERT_str.syntax_info: The INSERT(str, pos, len, newstr) function replaces a substring starting at position pos with length len characters with newstr. Returns NULL if any argument is NULL.

INSTR.syntax_info: The INSTR(str, substr) function returns the position of the first occurrence of substr in str. Returns 0 if not found, NULL if any argument is NULL.

LCASE.syntax_info: The LCASE(str) function is a synonym for LOWER(str). Returns the lowercase of the given argument. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

LEFT.syntax_info: The LEFT(str, len) function returns the leftmost len characters from str. Returns NULL if any argument is NULL.

LENGTH.syntax_info: The LENGTH() function returns the length of a string in bytes. LENGTH() counts a multibyte character as multiple bytes. If the argument is NULL, the function returns NULL.

like.syntax_info: The LIKE operator is used for simple string matching. The expression expr LIKE pat [ESCAPE 'escape_char'] returns 1 (TRUE) or 0 (FALSE). If either expr or pat is NULL, the result is NULL. Wildcards: % matches any number of characters, _ matches exactly one character.

LOCATE.syntax_info: The LOCATE(substr, str[, pos]) function returns the position of the first occurrence of substr in str. Returns 0 if not found, NULL if any argument is NULL.

LOWER.syntax_info: The LOWER(str) function converts all characters in str to lowercase. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

LPAD.syntax_info: The LPAD(str, len, padstr) function returns str left-padded with padstr to length len. Returns NULL if any argument is NULL.

LTRIM.syntax_info: The LTRIM() function removes leading spaces from a string. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

MAKE_SET.syntax_info: The MAKE_SET(bits, str1, str2, ...) function returns a set of comma-separated strings based on bits.

MID.syntax_info: The MID(str, pos, len) function returns a substring starting at position pos with length len. Synonym for SUBSTRING.

not_like.syntax_info: Negation of simple pattern matching. This function performs the inverse operation of LIKE. Returns 1 (TRUE) or 0 (FALSE).

not_regexp.syntax_info: Negation of REGEXP. Pattern matching using regular expressions. Returns 1 or 0.

OCT.syntax_info: The OCT() function converts a number to its octal string representation. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

OCTET_LENGTH.syntax_info: The OCTET_LENGTH() function returns the length of a string in bytes. Synonym for LENGTH().

ORD.syntax_info: The ORD() function returns the numeric value of the leftmost character. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

POSITION.syntax_info: The POSITION(substr IN str) function returns the position of substr in str. Returns 0 if not found.

QUOTE.syntax_info: The QUOTE() function returns a string enclosed in single quotes with proper escaping.

regexp.syntax_info: Pattern matching using regular expressions. Returns 1 (match) or 0 (no match).

REGEXP_INSTR.syntax_info: The REGEXP_INSTR(str, pattern) function returns the position of the first match of pattern in str.

REGEXP_LIKE.syntax_info: The REGEXP_LIKE(str, pattern) function returns 1 if str matches pattern, 0 otherwise.

REGEXP_REPLACE.syntax_info: The REGEXP_REPLACE(str, pattern, replacement) function replaces matches of pattern with replacement.

REGEXP_SUBSTR.syntax_info: The REGEXP_SUBSTR(str, pattern) function returns the substring matching pattern.

REPEAT.syntax_info: The REPEAT(str, count) function repeats the string str count times. Returns empty string if count is 0, NULL if any argument is NULL.

REPLACE.syntax_info: The REPLACE(str, from_str, to_str) function replaces all occurrences of from_str with to_str in str. Returns NULL if any argument is NULL.

REVERSE.syntax_info: The REVERSE(str) function returns the string str with the order of characters reversed. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

RIGHT.syntax_info: The RIGHT(str, len) function returns the rightmost len characters from str.

rlike.syntax_info: Synonym for REGEXP. Pattern matching using regular expressions. Returns 1 or 0.

RPAD.syntax_info: The RPAD(str, len, padstr) function returns str right-padded with padstr to length len.

RTRIM.syntax_info: The RTRIM() function removes trailing spaces from a string.

SPACE.syntax_info: The SPACE(N) function returns a string of N space characters.

SUBSTR.syntax_info: The SUBSTR(str, pos[, len]) function returns a substring starting at position pos with optional length len.

SUBSTRING.syntax_info: The SUBSTRING(str, pos[, len]) function returns a substring starting at position pos.

SUBSTRING_INDEX.syntax_info: The SUBSTRING_INDEX(str, delim, count) function returns a substring before/after count occurrences of delim.

TO_BASE64.syntax_info: The TO_BASE64() function encodes a string to Base64 format.

TRANSLATE.syntax_info: The TRANSLATE(str, from_str, to_str) function replaces characters in str based on mapping.

TRIM.syntax_info: The TRIM() function removes leading and trailing spaces from a string.

UCASE.syntax_info: The UCASE(str) function is a synonym for UPPER(). Converts string to uppercase.

UNHEX.syntax_info: The UNHEX(str) function converts a hexadecimal string to bytes. Returns NULL if argument is NULL.

UPPER.syntax_info: The UPPER(str) function converts all characters in str to uppercase.

WEIGHT_STRING.syntax_info: The WEIGHT_STRING(str) function returns the weight string for a character string.

CURRENT_RESOURCE_GROUP.syntax_info: The CURRENT_RESOURCE_GROUP() function returns the current resource group name.

TIDB_BOUNDED_STALENESS.syntax_info: TIDB_BOUNDED_STALENESS(from, to) returns a time range for stale reads.

TIDB_CURRENT_TSO.syntax_info: The TIDB_CURRENT_TSO() function returns the current TSO timestamp.

TIDB_DECODE_BINARY_PLAN.syntax_info: The TIDB_DECODE_BINARY_PLAN(binary_plan) function decodes binary plans, like the ones in the BINARY_PLAN column of the STATEMENTS_SUMMARY table.

TIDB_DECODE_KEY.syntax_info: The TIDB_DECODE_KEY(key) function decodes a TiDB key.

TIDB_DECODE_PLAN.syntax_info: The TIDB_DECODE_PLAN(plan) function decodes a TiDB execution plan.

TIDB_DECODE_SQL_DIGESTS.syntax_info: TIDB_DECODE_SQL_DIGESTS(digests) decodes SQL digests into SQL statements.

TIDB_ENCODE_SQL_DIGEST.syntax_info: The TIDB_ENCODE_SQL_DIGEST(digests) function encodes SQL digests.

TIDB_IS_DDL_OWNER.syntax_info: The TIDB_IS_DDL_OWNER() function returns whether the current instance is DDL owner.

TIDB_PARSE_TSO.syntax_info: The TIDB_PARSE_TSO(tso) function parses a TSO timestamp.

TIDB_PARSE_TSO_LOGICAL.syntax_info: The TIDB_PARSE_TSO_LOGICAL(tso) function returns the logical part of a TSO timestamp.

TIDB_SHARD.syntax_info: The TIDB_SHARD() function creates a shard index value.

TIDB_VERSION.syntax_info: The TIDB_VERSION() function returns the TiDB version string.

VITESS_HASH.syntax_info: The VITESS_HASH() function returns a Vitess hash value.

FORMAT_BYTES.syntax_info: The FORMAT_BYTES() function converts a number of bytes into a human-readable format.

FORMAT_NANO_TIME.syntax_info: The FORMAT_NANO_TIME() function converts a number of nanoseconds into a human-readable time format.
